title: US-Iran War Near End, Blockade in Place
standfirst: Trump announces war is 'very close to over' as blockade continues · European allies refuse Hormuz mission · Xi-Lavrov strengthen ties in Beijing

stories.headline: US-Iran War Near End, Blockade in Place
stories.body: Trump announced the war is "very close to over" as the US military maintains a full blockade on Iranian ports. Pakistani mediators arrived in Tehran to revive peace talks between Washington and Tehran.

stories.headline: European Allies Refuse Hormuz Blockade
stories.body: European governments declined to join the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, creating tensions with Washington while their economies face potential energy supply disruptions.

stories.headline: UK Won't Yield to Trump War Pressure
stories.body: PM Keir Starmer declared Britain "not going to yield" to Trump's threats over the Iran war, rejecting pressure to join military operations despite US warnings about the bilateral trade deal.

stories.headline: Xi-Lavrov Meeting Strengthens Ties
stories.body: Chinese President Xi Jinping met Russian FM Sergei Lavrov in Beijing, calling China-Russia relations "precious" and urging closer strategic coordination to defend mutual interests and Global South unity.

stories.headline: Japan's Drone Tech Boosts Ukraine
stories.body: Japanese Terra A1 interceptor drones are reshaping Ukraine's defense capabilities. Ukraine's deep strikes hit 70+ Russian industrial targets in March, including oil and gas facilities.

stories.headline: US-Hamas Hold Direct Gaza Talks
stories.body: The US and Hamas held their first direct talks since the Gaza truce as ceasefire negotiations stall. A flotilla carrying activists and aid sailed from Barcelona toward Gaza.

stories.headline: India-Germany Deepen Defense Ties
stories.body: Both nations agreed to strengthen cooperation in defense, green hydrogen, and critical technologies amid escalating Middle East crisis and global instability.

stories.headline: US Nears Net Crude Exporter Status Amid Iran War
stories.body: The US nearly turned into a net crude exporter last week for the first time since World War Two as shipments surged to meet demand from Asian and European buyers scrambling to replace Middle East supplies.
